data.title: French crypto firm Ledger seeks $4B U.S. IPO - report
data.description:
French hardware wallet and crypto security provider Ledger is working with several investment banks to facilitate a U.S. initial public offering this year that could value it at over $4B, the Financial Times reported, citing people familiar.

The move comes as the wave of crypto-related public debuts since pro-crypto President Donald Trump returned to the White House last year shows no signs of slowing. Earlier in the week, crypto custody firm BitGo (BTGO) shares started trading after raising about $213M at a $2.08B valuation. KRAKacquisition Corp, a new special-purpose acquisition company backed by crypto exchange Kraken (KRAKEN) said last week it's seeking to raise $250M through a U.S. IPO.

Ledger CEO Pascal Gauthier said in November that his company was weighing a U.S. IPO, the Financial Times had reported, pointing out that 2025 was on track for a record year, with revenue reaching the hundreds of millions.

Goldman Sachs (GS), Jefferies (JEF) and Barclays (BCS), the banks working on the Ledger deal, declined to comment, the FT said. Ledger also declined to comment.

data.title: FB Financial cut to Neutral at Piper Sandler
data.description:
FB Financial (FBK) was trading lower after Piper Sandler cut its recommendation on the stock, citing that investors have realized significant upside in the shares.

Shares were -1.15% Friday pre-market to $60.05.

"FBK delivered another solid quarter in 4Q25 to round out a very strong year. Management sentiment around 2026 trends (and beyond) were highly encouraging as well, and we continue to believe that FBK will be a multi-year winner as it comes to customer and talent acquisitions within its markets," said analysts Stephen Scouten and John Martin.

"That said, we think that the current share price largely recognizes this upside today, trading at ~12.5x our 2026E. We are increasing our 2026/7E's to $4.88/$5.55 on a higher NIM trajectory while increasing our PT to $65 (now ~13.5x our 2026E, from ~13x, given the run-up in bank valuations)," added the analysts.

Piper Sandler downgraded the stock to Neutral from Overweight, but increased the price target to $65.00 from $63.00.

The rating aligns with Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating of Hold. Meanwhile, the Wall Street analysts grade FBK as Buy.

data.title: Bausch Health falls on phase 3 failure of liver disease complications drug
data.description:
• Bausch Health Companies (BHC)  is down ~4% in premarket trading Friday after a pair of phase 3 trials of an asset for liver cirrhosis patients to prevent hepatic encephalopathy missed its primary goal.
  • The phase 3 RED-C clinical program was examining amorphous-rifaximin solid soluble dispersion to delay the first episode of hepatic encephalopathy.
  • Bausch Health did not provide detailed results from the trials.

data.title: Tesla's claims of 'unsupervised' Austin robotaxi challenged -- report
data.description:
Tesla’s (TSLA) assertion that its Austin robotaxis operate fully autonomously is being challenged by reports that the vehicle is accompanied by a trailing support car, suggesting it is not operating independently.

On Thursday, Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk posted on X that the company had “just started Tesla Robotaxi drives in Austin with no safety monitor in the car.” But according to electrek, the robotaxi is being followed by a trailing Tesla to provide supervision.

A  video on X.com shows a vehicle driving approximately 15-20 feet behind the robotaxi, following the robotaxi to its destination.

“Here’s the reality: Tesla didn’t remove the safety monitor. It simply moved them from inside the robotaxi to a chase car following closely behind,” electrek said, accusing Musk of making misleading—though not false—claims about the autonomy of the robotaxi.

Since Tesla (TSLA) has not yet provided any information as to what the trailing vehicle is there for, videos posted online suggest a human is in the vehicle to provide assistance in case of an emergency or failure of the autonomous technology, thereby putting into question the company’s claims that the robotaxi is operating “unsupervised.”

The race for fully autonomous robotaxis between Waymo (GOOG), Zoox (AMZN), Tesla (TSLA) and Chinese competitors has led to the rapid deployment of vehicles in major U.S. cities and in the Middle East, where Chinese companies like WeRide, Baidu (BIDU), and Pony.ai (PONY) dominate, while in the U.S., Waymo (GOOG) leads. To catch Waymo’s (GOOG) lead, competitors are developing partnerships, moving into less regulated markets overseas, rapidly scaling service in U.S. cities, and even expanding the definition of "fully autonomous."

data.title: KNOREX appoints Michael Sun as CFO
data.description:
• KNOREX (KNRX) said on Friday it has appointed Ning (Michael) Sun as Chief Financial Officer, effective January 22, 2026.
  • Sun previously served as head of capital markets at KNOREX, where he led capital markets planning, investor engagement and pre-IPO initiatives.
  • Before joining the company, he held senior roles at U.S.-listed companies Jiayin Group and Fang Holdings, including Vice President of capital markets at Jiayin Group and Director of investor relations at Fang Holdings.
  • KNOREX said Sun’s appointment strengthens its executive leadership team as it builds internal infrastructure to support its next phase of growth as a public company.
  • JFIN +1.98% premarket to $7.21.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Booz Allen Hamilton trades higher on raised fiscal 2026 profitability outlook
data.description:
Booz Allen Hamilton (BAH) was trading higher after the advanced technology company raised its fiscal 2026 profitability outlook.

Shares were +6.70% Friday pre-market to $102.18.

The company delivered non-GAAP EPS of $1.77 (+14.2% Y/Y), surpassing Wall Street consensus by $0.50. Revenue of $2.6B (-10.2% Y/Y) misses estimates by $130M.

As the FQ3 revenue decline was offset by an increase in profitability, Booz Allen Hamilton upwardly revised its fiscal 2026 adjusted EPS guidance range to $5.95-$6.15 from $5.45-$5.65.

The fiscal 2026 adjusted EPS consensus estimate stands at $5.57.

Meanwhile, the revenue guidance range was narrowed to $11.3B-$11.4B from $11.3B-$11.5B. The consensus stands at $11.35B. For fiscal 2025, revenue had come at $12.0B.

"Booz Allen's third quarter results are on track and reflect disciplined execution in a dynamic environment," said CEO Horacio Rozanski.

data.title: OLB Group raises ~$1.3M in registered offering with warrants
data.description:
• OLB Group (OLB) entered a securities purchase agreement to sell 2.17M shares of common stock at $0.60/share, raising ~$1.3M before fees.
  • The offering includes a concurrent private placement of warrants to purchase up to 2.17M additional shares.
  • The warrants carry an exercise price of $0.78/share, become exercisable six months after issuance, and have a five-year term.
  • The transaction is expected to close on or about January 26, 2026.

data.title: Canada's retail sales likely fell 0.5% in December
data.description:
• Retail sales in Canada decreased 0.50 percent in December of 2025 over the previous month. 
  • Retail Sales in Canada increased 3.10 percent in November of 2025 over the same month in the previous year.
